sentences.text: Pretty cool.
Good morning, boss.
How are you today?
Hey, what's up, Phil?
How you doing, man?
I'm doing great!
I'm doing great.
I woke up on the right side of the bed.
I'm in the green recovery zone.
Everything's going pretty hunky-dory this morning.
Nice, man, I'm glad to hear that, I'm… I was having a little rough morning on my end.
No internet, so I'm at the park, on the Wi-Fi, and hoping it's… It's on the next hour or so, so….
Rough start for sure, but at least you got that fresh air.
For sure, yeah, I'm, chillin' here with the dogs, so it could be worse, for sure.
Awesome.
Well, thanks for joining.
I'm sure you probably saw Corey's not able to make it.
Oh, shoot, I didn't see that.
Okay.
Sorry, I haven't even looked at my Slack.
No worries, I'm sure your Slack is a mess first thing in the morning.
But yeah, Corey can't make it, because I guess he's working on a UDR demo or proposal of some sort.
Okay.

But what he was mentioning, was that one of the most important things, I guess, is just, letting William know that they have additional features, full access to research and explore, and then we have to set up another call for data sync?
Which I guess we'll have our technical resources on, but you know, anything else that you think… William definitely sounds like he has some questions.
He was really keen to schedule a call, and to get scheduled a call before the month was up.
So… I was really gonna go in there, kinda hear what his concerns were.
and try to figure out who's gonna be in charge of this whole thing, because I can't imagine his scope is growing very, very much beyond… right, here come new properties, we need to get them in IQ.
I think I need… I think I still need to understand who's gonna be calling the shots, or who's gonna be running… the research teams, and their access, the data teams and their access, right?
So….
Okay.
I think that's something.
That's what I know.
Like, kind of some general discovery, like, hey, how are.
Yeah.
wanting this to work?
Who should be the main POCs for each of these?
That seems like a good place to start.
Right, and then just using this as, like, okay, we're gonna set the expectations, I need a call with this person and that person, and we'll do that next week, and so on and so on.
Yeah, I like that.
I mean, did they ever actually sign the contract renewal?

Like, that's… I wasn't totally clear, because I know we were, like, waiting on the contract guy, and then Corey pasted that… I think maybe it was just to me and George, or all of us, I can't remember.
some VP or something was like, oh, we're really excited to keep working with you, so I'm like, I don't know what exactly is going on.
That was Cynthia, I've got Cynthia right here on the 20th.
Oh, nice, okay, so they did sign the contract.

Great, okay, I didn't know if that meant it was signed, or if it just… they were working on it, so that's fantastic, and so it sounds like it's now just, hey, talking about the rollout, and how we get, you know, them enabled for all these new features.
Definitely.
Exactly right.
Cool.
Awesome.
For some reason, I was thinking, like, there's still contract details to iron out, but this is even better.
That's great.
Well, it was news to me.
Well, I think I finally caught the message, like, Tuesday afternoon, Kristen sent me the contract and said, by the way, this got signed, congrats.
Ugh, God.
Common to myself on that one.
Yeah, I don't know why we can't talk to each other when stuff like this happens, but….
Yeah.
Whatever.
Okay, well, I mean, is there anything you feel like you need support-wise at this point?
Or is it just, like, hey, you gotta talk to William, figure out kinda what their game plan is?
I think I gotta talk to William and figure out what the game plan is, because I don't see much changing about my relationship with William.
Sure.
I think now, it's like, how do we break out of these silos, right?
And how do I get really comfortable talking to… whether it's the acquisitions team, the investment team, whoever the hell is gonna be using the research reports most often.
You know, how do… who are those people, and how do I talk to them?
Yeah.
How do we get them acclimated and up and running?
How do we get the data sync up and running and in the right hands?
Because right now, you know, a few people have it, and we're not 100% sure who is pulling in all the time.
Okay.
So I really think this is just kind of a mapping discovery call, set some expectations for future calls.
Okay.
Perfect, man.
Well, that seems pretty straightforward to me, I mean….
Yeah.
Yeah, that makes total sense.
I don't even know that we necessarily needed this sink, because it sounds like you got it well in hand under control.
I'm sure Cory just wanted to be super sure that I was up to speed on everything that got signed.
To be honest, I'm not 100% sure even what the details are of what… you know, do they have unlimited research seats?
I don't actually know that for sure, I gotta ask him.
Because it doesn't say so in this contract that I'm reading, I think.
What…
Access they have.
I mean, I know that's what we talked about with… I keep forgetting his name, the contract guy.
David.
Yeah, like, hey, we would give it to everybody.
So, yeah, let's just plan on that, like, hey.
Like, unlimited, right?
Yes, we want to give it to everybody that wants it.
You know, I think… Just in general, like.
most of our accounts were gonna do this for them, like, give them research, you know, we keep hearing it that, like.
I don't even quite know the best way to describe it, but if there's, like, a negative piece of feedback about our product versus HelloData.

It's almost like… Again, I don't really know quite a way to frame it, but… It's almost just like a… an enablement or education thing, like, if you look in the HelloData system, and Alec was showing me a few weeks ago, like, the way it's set up.
It kind of feels like it's really simple to certain users, because research and market survey are basically the same thing.
There's no delineation.
You just… it literally, like, there's a window right when you go in, and you can start making market surveys right from there.
Wow.
So, like, Scotland, as an example.

… you know, they didn't… we had research turned on for them, but they didn't really understand what it was, or where it was within the system, and they're looking at Hello Day, and they're like, oh, well, we can just go in here and do whatever the fuck we want, create as.
Yeah.
surveys as we want, your guy's system is inferior, and it's like, no, not at all.
You just have to go to a different button to get to this thing, or this function.
So, I think… What we've been talking about for the last months or so is, like, hey, long-term, we probably need to combine market survey and research into basic.
Yeah.
one tab, so nobody gets confused, and there's no question about, like, is this a parity with Hello Data?
And then, okay, if you want the sub-market data, i.
e.
explore, cool, that's a tab, you can go there.
And then if you want the rev management or the insights component, cool, that's a tab, you can go there.
So I think that should help, and I think just in the meantime.
You know, hey, anything we can do to help these customers understand, whether it's training, or hey, if we need to build some enablement documents for them, videos, you know.

you tell us what William thinks would be helpful, but we can pull Jamie and Sarah in to build specific content for them, or, you know, run a bunch of training sessions to make sure their teams understand how to use this stuff.
Absolutely right.

I think we kind of want to treat this, like, as a… as a new customer, in a way, and, like, a redo the… and I feel like we talked about this a little bit, maybe Q2, when we were first getting into how risky this renewal could be, but we need to kind of re-run that onboarding playbook, and so I'll definitely be asking him what support he thinks he needs, and see if we need to tap Jamie and Sarah as well.

I think that's the right move, man, yeah, almost treating like it's a restart, and, you know, now that we've got all these new people involved, and new functionality for them, and hopefully we can just you know, pour a ton of value on them, and they'll continue to be super happy, because, I mean, all the feedback we got at NAA, I mean, was… extremely positive.
So I think if we just keep that momentum going and get these guys enabled with the new stuff, we should be in really good shape.
Yeah, that sounds good.
I'm psyched!
Let's fucking do it.
Cool, man.
Come on.
Alright.
Love it, man.

Well, glad you're feeling good, and yeah, anything you need after the call, give me a holler, but … yeah, otherwise, man, I guess I'll give you a tie back, unless there's anything else you want to talk about.
No, I think I'm all set.
Thanks, boss, I'll talk to you in a few.
Okay, sounds good, man.
There you see it.
